title: laportea peduncularis subsp. peduncularis
intro: Laportea peduncularis subsp. peduncularis, also known as the American wood nettle, is a perennial plant species in the nettle family (Urticaceae) native to eastern North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, to treat various health conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and skin irritations. The plant is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Laportea peduncularis subsp. peduncularis can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and topical creams, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of chronic conditions and promote overall well-being.
